session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/async-kindling-dawn.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Fix: Revert URL quoting in Rich `[link=...]` markup — quotes break OSC 8 links Current context: (wt:erk-slot-38) (br:plnd/align-plan-dash-02-22-1844) (pr:#7937) (plan:#7937)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/alig"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create a plan PR" (Recommended) - Create a draft PR with the plan and stop. Does NOT proceed to implementation. 2. "Skip PR and implement here" - Skip creating a PR, implement directly in current worktree (for small PR iterations that don't need PR tracking). 3. "View/Edit the plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create a plan PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. STOP - Do NOT call ExitPlanMode. The plan-save command handles everything. Stay in plan mode and let the user exit manually if desired.
If user chooses 'Skip PR and implement here': 1. Create implement-now marker (skip PR creation): erk exec marker create --session-id ce62ecac-3aa2-47ec-a421-a77c258c3e19 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly (no PR tracking - this is for small PR iterations)
... [5 lines omitted] ...

1→# Fix: Revert URL quoting in Rich `[link=...]` markup — quotes break OSC 8 links 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→The previous plan incorrectly added double quotes around URLs in Rich `[link=...]` markup. This was based on `docs/learned/textual/quirks.md` which documents quoting requirements for **Textual widgets** (e.g., `Label(..., markup=True)`), NOT for Rich Console markup. 6→ 7→**Root cause analysis** (traced through Rich source): 8→1. `markup.py:99` — `tag_text.partition("=")` extracts the raw value after `=` as `parameters` 9→2. `markup.py:217` — `str(tag)` produces `"link URL"` (or `'link "URL"'` if quoted) 10→3. `style.py:515` — `style_definition.split()` splits by whitespace 11→4. `style.py:543` — `link = word` assigns the next word verbatim as the URL 12→ 13→With quotes: `[link="https://github.com/..."]` → OSC 8 gets `"https://..."` (literal quotes in URL) → **invalid URL, not clickable** 14→Without quotes: `[link=https://github.com/...]` → OSC 8 gets `https://...` → **valid URL, clickable** 15→ 16→Confirmed by Rich's own example (`markup.py:239`): `[link=https://www.willmcgugan.com]here[/link]` 17→Confirmed by working reference impl (`view_cmd.py:68`): `f"[link={repo_base_url}/issues/{issue_number}]..."` 18→ 19→## Changes 20→ 21→### 1. Revert URL quoting in `_row_to_static_values()` — `src/erk/cli/commands/plan/list_cmd.py` 22→ 23→Remove quotes around URLs in all four link cells: 24→ 25→- **Plan cell** (line 189): `f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"` (revert quotes) 26→- **Objective cell** (line 193-196): `f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"` (keep link, remove quotes) 27→- **Run-id** (line 209): `f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"` (revert quotes) 28→- **PR display** (line 236): `f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"` (revert quotes) 29→ 30→### 2. Revert URL quoting in `format_pr_cell()` — `src/erk/cli/commands/plan/list_cmd.py`
... [30 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Fix: Revert URL quoting in Rich `[link=...]` markup — quotes break OSC 8 ...[truncated 522 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

What would you like to do with this plan?"="Skip PR and implement here". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

tool_use.param: erk exec marker create --session-id ce62ecac-3aa2-47ec-a421-a77c258c3e19 exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now
